Scenario.title: Honeybee Operation: Phishing-Driven Data Disruption in Communications Sector
Scenario.overview: Communications Simple A sophisticated phishing campaign targeting communications infrastructure leverages compromised email accounts to deliver malware through public-facing applications, ultimately manipulating critical data to disrupt operations.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Application Log, OS Process
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Application Log Content (Mechanism), Data Manipulation

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker initiates contact through compromised email accounts, deploying phishing emails designed to trick recipients into executing malicious payloads. This establishes the initial foothold in the communications network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: Malware delivered via phishing is executed to exploit vulnerabilities in communication software's application logs. Attackers manipulate log content to bypass authentication mechanisms and gain unauthorized access to systems.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Critical communication data is altered through OS-level processes, disrupting service integrity. Attackers modify data formats and structures to corrupt operational workflows and degrade network performance.

Scenario.summary: A targeted phishing campaign exploits weaknesses in communications infrastructure through public-facing applications, culminating in data manipulation attacks that compromise network reliability and operational continuity.
